result:
By default, React re-renders every child when the parent re-renders — regardless of whether props actually changed. Reference equality is the gotcha: new object/array/function literals on each parent render look like "new props" to memo checks.

Common causes:

1. **Not memoized.** Wrap the child in `React.memo(Child)` so it skips re-renders when props are shallowly equal.
2. **New references each render.** `<Child onClick={() => ...} data={{x:1}} />` creates fresh identities every time, defeating `React.memo`. Fix with `useCallback` / `useMemo` in the parent.
3. **Context consumers.** Any component reading a context re-renders when the provider's `value` changes — and `value={{ ... }}` inline creates a new object every render.
4. **Children as props.** `<Parent><Child/></Parent>` — if `Parent` re-renders, the `children` element is re-created unless the JSX is lifted to a stable ancestor.

Quickest diagnosis: open React DevTools → Profiler → "Why did this render?" It tells you which prop changed identity.

If you share the component, I can point at the exact cause.
